length_parity_notes: All 5 questions: correct/mean ratio — q1:1.06, q2:1.03, q3:1.01, q4:1.12, q5:1.14 — all within 1.20 threshold

questions.objective_ref: LO1 — why an LLM produces a confident-sounding answer even when that answer is false
LO2 — what next-token prediction is and how it drives both correct and incorrect outputs
LO3 — distinguishing intrinsic hallucination from extrinsic hallucination
LO4 — why an LLM cannot simply say I don't know by default
LO5 — real-world harm domains and why confidence makes hallucination worse
